# Viewpulse Feature Prioritization

**Generated by:** CTO  
**Date:** 2026-04-27  
**Project:** Viewpulse (Project #28)

---

## Current MVP State (✅ Live)

- YouTube OAuth connection
- Dashboard with 4 metric cards (views, watch time, subscribers, avg duration)
- Views over time line chart
- Top 10 videos bar chart
- Video table with sorting + pagination
- Date range selector (7d / 28d / 90d)
- Adoption analytics (in-memory, resets on deploy)
- Retry + backoff for quota errors
- 1-hr in-memory caches

---

## Proposed Next Features

### 🥇 Priority 1: Video Comparison
**Why:** High impact, moderate effort. Core differentiator vs YouTube Studio.

**What:**
- Select 2-10 videos from table (checkbox column)
- Side-by-side comparison view: views, CTR, avg duration, likes, comments, date
- Normalized % bars (best performer = 100%)
- Export to clipboard button

**API cost:** Low (reuse already-fetched video stats from existing `getVideos` call)

**Effort:** ~4h  
**Quota impact:** Negligible (data already cached or fetched once)

**Blocking:** None — can start immediately.

---

### 🥈 Priority 2: Trends Detection
**Why:** Medium effort, unlocks "stickiness" — users check back daily for trend insights.

**What:**
- Compute 7-day rolling averages for views/watch time
- Detect "rising" / "falling" videos vs prior period
- Show trend arrows on video table (↑ ↓ →)
- Optional: email digest with top movers (post-MVP)

**API cost:** Low (analytics API already used for daily views)

**Effort:** ~6h  
**Quota impact:** Low (reuse cached analytics data)

**Dependencies:** Requires Analytics API (not available for all channels — has fallback).

---

### 🥉 Priority 3: Threshold Alerts
**Why:** Drives retention. Most requested by early creator users.

**What:**
- User sets thresholds per metric (e.g., "alert me if views < 1000 in 7 days")
- Stored in localStorage (MVP) or Supabase (v2)
- Dashboard badge shows alert count
- Alert panel on dashboard
- Future: email notification (post-MVP)

**API cost:** None (evaluate on cached data)

**Effort:** ~3h for localStorage version  
**Quota impact:** None

**UX note:** Needs onboarding flow to set thresholds (2-3 screens).

---

## Not Recommended for MVP+1

| Feature | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| Competitor benchmarking | Requires scraping / third-party data, high effort |
| AI insights | Hallucination risk, no data to train on, not differentiated |
| PDF/PNG export | PDF libraries add bundle bloat, low frequency |
| Dark mode | Aesthetic only, no retention impact at this stage |
| Mobile PWA | Low traffic, bad ROI at early validation stage |

---

## Recommended Execution Order

1. **Video Comparison** — Quick win, high delight factor, immediate value
2. **Trends** — Medium effort, builds habit (users come back daily)
3. **Threshold Alerts** — Low effort, high retention impact

---

## Dependencies

- Video Comparison: None (uses existing video stats already fetched)
- Trends: Requires Analytics API (may fallback gracefully to zeros)
- Alerts: None (client-side localStorage evaluation)

---

## CTO Recommendation

Ship **Video Comparison** as the single highest-ROI next feature. It requires:
- New `components/VideoCompare.tsx`
- New `app/api/compare/route.ts` (optional, could be client-side)
- Checkbox column added to `VideoTable.tsx`
- Comparison modal/slideout

Then move to Trends (uses same analytics data, higher stickiness value).
